attempts.strategy: Drop in the iter-014 plan-agent's probe-confirmed term-mode one-liner verbatim into the noncomputable abbrev body at L236; uses qualified `Abelian.Ext` spelling consistent with iter-009 `HModule` and iter-010 `HModule_zero_linearEquiv`.
attempts.goal_before: term-position ⊢ Type u under binders {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))] (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C)
attempts.insight: The term `(yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X` produces a presheaf of k-modules on C (the free k-module on the Yoneda-representable presheaf at X); sheafify it via `presheafToSheaf` to get a sheaf in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; then `Abelian.Ext _ F n` against the target sheaf F yields the n-th cohomology of the open X with coefficients in F. The `noncomputable abbrev` reducibility surfaces `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances on the resulting `Abelian.Ext` value through the `Linear k` enrichment chain. Probe-confirmed verbatim by the iter-014 plan agent; first-edit closure.
